Group_Project

Ruolin Huang,Weifan Liu, Hangjun Liu

2026-03-12

Group members’ names: Ruolin Huang, Hangjun Liu, Weifan Liu #DATA: For this porject.We use the data from Thodoris Danis and Antonis Rokas’ research.FROM:Danis T, Rokas A.2024 The evolution of gestation length in eutherian mammals.Proc.R.Soc.B291:20241412.Links:https://doi.org/10.1098/rspb.2024.1412

https://github.com/Ruolin030/BIS15L_W2026_Group10

library("tidyverse")
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.6
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.1     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
## ✔ purrr     1.2.1     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library("naniar")
library("janitor")
## 
## Attaching package: 'janitor'
## 
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     chisq.test, fisher.test
library("ggthemes")
library("RColorBrewer")
library("paletteer")
library("ggplot2")
library("shiny")

1.load data.

gestation<- read_csv("data/Danis and Rokas 2024 Gestation Length/Data_S1.csv") %>% clean_names()
## Rows: 845 Columns: 5
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (2): Species, Order
## dbl (3): Bodymass, Longevity, Gestationength
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
  1. Exploration of the Dataset
glimpse(gestation)
## Rows: 845
## Columns: 5
## $ species        <chr> "Erethizon dorsatum", "Coendou prehensilis", "Dinomys b…
## $ order          <chr> "Rodentia", "Rodentia", "Rodentia", "Rodentia", "Rodent…
## $ bodymass       <dbl> 7419.46, 4116.20, 12500.00, 1220.00, 1539.99, 499.99, 4…
## $ longevity      <dbl> 18.00, 27.00, 13.00, 19.50, 19.50, 17.50, 10.00, 9.40, …
## $ gestationength <dbl> 210.99, 203.00, 253.00, 140.00, 134.00, 111.00, 111.00,…
summary(gestation)
##    species             order              bodymass           longevity     
##  Length:845         Length:845         Min.   :        2   Min.   :  1.17  
##  Class :character   Class :character   1st Qu.:      138   1st Qu.:  8.60  
##  Mode  :character   Mode  :character   Median :     3200   Median : 16.00  
##                                        Mean   :   691437   Mean   : 18.58  
##                                        3rd Qu.:    35120   3rd Qu.: 24.00  
##                                        Max.   :154321304   Max.   :200.00  
##  gestationength
##  Min.   : 16   
##  1st Qu.: 40   
##  Median :120   
##  Mean   :135   
##  3rd Qu.:190   
##  Max.   :517
dim(gestation)
## [1] 845   5
  1. Data Integrity and Quality Check Before proceeding to analysis, we must ensure there are no hidden missing values or logical errors.
gestation %>% 
  tabyl(order)
##               order   n     percent
##        Afrosoricida   6 0.007100592
##        Artiodactyla 156 0.184615385
##           Carnivora 121 0.143195266
##             Cetacea  44 0.052071006
##           Cingulata   6 0.007100592
##        Eulipotyphla  35 0.041420118
##          Lagomorpha  19 0.022485207
##   New_World_monkeys  40 0.047337278
##   Old_World_monkeys  53 0.062721893
##      Perissodactyla  15 0.017751479
##              Pilosa   8 0.009467456
##           Pinnipeds  26 0.030769231
##          Prosimians  35 0.041420118
##            Rodentia 220 0.260355030
##     Yangochiroptera  33 0.039053254
##  Yinpterochiroptera  28 0.033136095
gestation %>% 
  miss_var_summary()
## # A tibble: 5 × 3
##   variable       n_miss pct_miss
##   <chr>           <int>    <num>
## 1 species             0        0
## 2 order               0        0
## 3 bodymass            0        0
## 4 longevity           0        0
## 5 gestationength      0        0
names(gestation)
## [1] "species"        "order"          "bodymass"       "longevity"     
## [5] "gestationength"
gestation %>% 
  filter(bodymass == 0 | gestationength == 0 | longevity == 0)
## # A tibble: 0 × 5
## # ℹ 5 variables: species <chr>, order <chr>, bodymass <dbl>, longevity <dbl>,
## #   gestationength <dbl>

The diagnostic check confirms that the dataset is highly complete, with no missing values (NAs) or illogical zero-entries in the primary variables #LHJ

avg_longevity <- gestation %>%
  group_by(order) %>%
  summarise(mean_longevity = mean(longevity, na.rm = TRUE))
avg_longevity %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = order, y=mean_longevity))+
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "darkblue") +
  labs(title = "Average Longevity by Mammal Order",
       x = "order",
       y = "Average Longevity (years)")+
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

#Weifan Liu 3. Briefly understand the datasets. 3.1 Distrubution of order

order_dist <- gestation %>% 
  count(order) %>% 
  mutate(order=fct_reorder(order,n))
my_color <- c("#F7A6AC","#FC757B","#F97F5F","#FFA288","#FAA26F","#FDCD94","#FEE199","#FFD47D","#B0D6A9","#6AD1A3","#65BDBA","#3C9BC9","#7FBDDA","#84ADDC","#B8E5FA","#CBE4B1")
order_dist %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=order,y=n,fill=order))+
  geom_col(color="white",linewidth=0.2)+
  coord_flip()+
  geom_text(aes(label=n),hjust=-0.2,size=3)+
  scale_fill_manual(values=my_color)+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(legend.position="none",
        panel.grid.minor=element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.y=element_blank())+
  labs(title="Taxonomic Composition of the Mammal Dataset",
       subtitle="Analysis of 845 species organized by Order",
       x="Order",
       y="Number of Species")

3.2 Do larger mammals have longer pregnancies?

gestation %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=log10(bodymass),
             y=log10(gestationength)))+
  geom_point(aes(color=order),alpha=0.6,size=1.5)+
  geom_smooth(method="lm",color="black",linetype="dashed",se=TRUE)+
  theme_minimal()+
  scale_color_manual(values = my_color)+
  labs(title="The Allometric Relationship of Gestation Length",
       subtitle="Log-linear relationship across 845 mammalian species",
       x="Log10 Body Mass (g)",
       y="Log10 Gestation Length (days)",
       color="Order")+
  theme(legend.position="right")
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

3.3 Faceting by Order

major_orders <- 
  gestation %>% 
  count(order) %>% 
  filter(n>=20) %>% 
  pull(order)
gestation %>% 
  filter(order %in% major_orders) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=log10(bodymass),
             y=log10(gestationength),
             color=order))+
  geom_point(alpha=0.6,size=1)+
  facet_wrap(~order,scale="free")+
  scale_color_manual(values=my_color)+
  theme_light()+
  theme(legend.position="none",
        strip.text=element_text(face="bold",color="black"),
        strip.background=element_rect(fill="grey90"))+
  labs(title="Comparing the slopes across major mammalian groups as discussed in the paper",
       x="Log10 Body Mass(g)",
       y="log10 Gestation Length(days")

gestation %>% 
  filter(order %in% major_orders) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=longevity,
             y=log10(gestationength),
             color=order))+
  geom_point(alpha=0.6,size=1)+
  facet_wrap(~order,scale="free")+
  scale_color_manual(values=my_color)+
  theme_light()+
  theme(legend.position="none",
        strip.text=element_text(face="bold",color="black"),
        strip.background=element_rect(fill="grey90"))+
  labs(title="Comparing the slopes across major mammalian groups as discussed in the paper",
       x="Longevity",
       y="log10 Gestation Length(days")

3.4 Life-history strategies （Do long-lived orders, such as primates, spend a higher percentage of their lifespan during pregnancy than those fast-dying rodents?）

gestation_investment <- 
  gestation %>% 
  filter(order %in% major_orders) %>% 
  mutate(invest_ratio=(gestationength/360)/longevity*100)
gestation_investment %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=fct_reorder(order,invest_ratio, .desc=TRUE),
             y=invest_ratio,
             fill=order))+
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape=NA,alpha=0.7)+
  coord_flip()+
  scale_fill_manual(values=my_color)+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(legend.position="none")+
  labs(title="Reproductive Investment Across Mammalian Orders",
       subtitle="Percentage of lifespan spent in a single gestation period",
       x="Order",
       y="Gestation as % of Total Lifespan")

3.5 Body Size vs. Pregnancy’s “Extreme Contrast Map”

extreme <- gestation %>% 
  filter(!is.na(bodymass)) %>% 
  mutate(category = case_when(
    rank(bodymass) <= 10 ~ "Tiny",
    rank(-bodymass) <= 10 ~ "Giant",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  )) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(category))
extreme %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=fct_reorder(species,bodymass),
             y=gestationength,
             fill=category))+
  geom_col(width=0.7)+
  coord_flip()+
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("Giant"="#F7B2C7",
                             "Tiny"="#B8E5FA"))+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(axis.text.y=element_text(face="italic",size=9),
        legend.position="top",
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank())+
  labs(title="The Great Gestation Gap",
       subtitle="Comparing the 10 largest and 10 smallest mammals in the dataset",
       x=NULL,
       y="Gestation Length (Days)",
       fill="Group")

#LHJ

library(shiny)
library(tidyverse)

ui <- fluidPage(
  titlePanel("Mammalian Gestation Predictor"),
  sidebarLayout(
    sidebarPanel(
      helpText("Adjust parameters to predict gestation length."),
      sliderInput("mass", "Body Mass (g):",
                  min = 2, max = 20000, value = 500, step = 10),
      sliderInput("longevity", "Lifespan (years):",
                  min = 1, max = 200, value = 40),
      selectInput("order_select",
                  "Select Mammal Order:",
                  choices = c("All", sort(unique(gestation$order))),
                  selected = "All"),
      textOutput("equation")),
    mainPanel(
      wellPanel(
        textOutput("prediction_text")),
      checkboxInput("show_lm",
                    "Show best-fit line (lm)",
                    value = FALSE),
      plotOutput("distPlot", width = "500px", height = "400px"))))

server <- function(input, output) {
  model <- lm(log(gestationength) ~ log(bodymass) + log(longevity),
              data = gestation)
  output$equation <- renderText({
    "log(gestation) = β0 + β1 log(bodymass) + β2 log(longevity)"})
  output$prediction_text <- renderText({new_data <- data.frame(
    bodymass = input$mass,
    longevity = input$longevity)
  pred_log <- predict(model, newdata = new_data)
  pred_days <- round(exp(pred_log), 1)
  paste0("Predicted Gestation Length: ", pred_days, " days")})
  output$distPlot <- renderPlot({
    current_pred_val <- exp(
      predict(model,
              data.frame(bodymass = input$mass,
                         longevity = input$longevity)))
    filtered_data <- gestation
    if (input$order_select != "All") {
      filtered_data <- gestation %>%
        filter(order == input$order_select)}
    if (input$order_select == "All") {
      p <- filtered_data %>%
        ggplot(aes(x = bodymass, y = gestationength, color = order)) +
        geom_point(alpha = 0.5)}
    else {
      p <- filtered_data %>%
        ggplot(aes(x = bodymass, y = gestationength)) +
        geom_point(color ="orange", size = 2) +
        geom_text(aes(label = species),
                  size = 2,
                  vjust = -0.7)}
    p <- p +
      scale_x_log10() +
      annotate("point",
               x = input$mass,
               y = current_pred_val,
               color = "black",
               size = 2,
               shape = 18) +
      labs(title = "Gestation vs Body Mass+lifesapn",
           subtitle = "Black diamond indicates your prediction",
           x = "Body Mass+lifesapn (log scale)",
           y = "Gestation Length") +
      theme_bw()
    
    if (input$show_lm) {p + geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE)} else {
      p }})}
shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server)

#Ruolin Huang

library(shiny)

ui <- fluidPage(
  titlePanel("Mammalian Gestation Predictor"),
  
  sidebarLayout(
    sidebarPanel(
      helpText("Adjust parameters to predict gestation length."),
      
      sliderInput("mass", "Body Mass (g):", min = 2, max = 2000, value = 70, step = 10),
      sliderInput("longevity", "Lifespan (years):", min = 1, max = 200, value = 20),
      selectInput("order", "Taxonomic Order:", 
                  choices = sort(unique(gestation$order))),
      
      textOutput("equation")
    ),
    
    mainPanel(
      wellPanel(
        textOutput("prediction_text")
      ),
      
       checkboxInput("show_lm",
                "show best-fit line(lm)",
                value=FALSE),
      
      plotOutput("distPlot", width="500px", height="400px")
    )
  )
)

server <- function(input, output) {
  
  model <- lm(log(gestationength) ~ log(bodymass) + log(longevity), data = gestation)
  
  output$equation <- renderText({
    "log(gestation) = β₀ + β₁(log bodymass) + β₂(log longevity) + order"
  })
  
  output$prediction_text <- renderText({
    
    new_data <- data.frame(
      bodymass = input$mass, 
      longevity = input$longevity, 
      order = input$order
    )
    
    pred_log <- predict(model, newdata = new_data)
    pred_days <- round(exp(pred_log), 1)
    
    paste0("Predicted Gestation Length: ", pred_days, " days")
  })
  
  output$distPlot <- renderPlot({
     #current_prediction_value
    current_pred_val <- exp(predict(model, data.frame(bodymass=input$mass, # exp() gives the actual predicted gestation length in days
                                                      longevity=input$longevity, 
                                                      order=input$order)))
    
    gestation %>%
      ggplot(aes(x = bodymass, y = gestationength))+
      geom_point(alpha = 0.5, aes(color = order))+
      scale_x_log10()+
      annotate("point", x = input$mass, y = current_pred_val, 
               color = "black", size = 5, shape = 18) +
      labs(title = "Gestation vs Body Mass (Log Scale)",
           subtitle = "Black diamond indicates your current prediction",
           x = "Body Mass (g)", y = "Gestation Length (days)")+
      theme_bw()+
      if (input$show_lm)
      geom_smooth(method="lm", se=F, na.rm=T)
  else
    NULL
    
  })
}

shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server)

#LHJ

ui <- pageWithSidebar(
  
  headerPanel("Species Finder by Traits"),
  
  sidebarPanel(
    selectInput("order",
                "Select Order",
                choices = c("All", sort(unique(gestation$order))),
                selected = "All"),
    
    sliderInput("bodymass",
                "Body Mass",
                min = min(gestation$bodymass, na.rm = TRUE),
                max = max(gestation$bodymass, na.rm = TRUE),
                value = range(gestation$bodymass, na.rm = TRUE)),
    
    sliderInput("longevity",
                "Longevity",
                min = min(gestation$longevity, na.rm = TRUE),
                max = max(gestation$longevity, na.rm = TRUE),
                value = range(gestation$longevity, na.rm = TRUE)),
    
    sliderInput("gestation",
                "Gestation Length",
                min = min(gestation$gestationength, na.rm = TRUE),
                max = max(gestation$gestationength, na.rm = TRUE),
                value = range(gestation$gestationength, na.rm = TRUE))
  ),
  
  mainPanel(
    tableOutput("species_table")
  )
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  
filtered_species <- reactive({
    data <- gestation
    if(input$order != "All"){data <- data %>% filter(order == input$order)}%>%
      filter(bodymass >= input$bodymass[1] & bodymass <= input$bodymass[2],
             longevity >= input$longevity[1] & longevity <= input$longevity[2],
             gestationength >= input$gestation[1] & gestationength <= input$gestation[2])})
output$species_table <- renderTable({filtered_species()})}

shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server)
